messages.content: You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system should be fast

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS - Quality Score 25/100

Review: The requirement uses non-quantifiable language. The term 'fast' lacks measurable criteria and is dependent on context. No baseline performance metric is established. Without definition, 'fast' could mean <100ms to <5 seconds depending on system context.

Recommendation: Replace subjective language with specific, measurable response time targets. Include performance conditions (normal load, peak load). Define percentile latency (p50, p95, p99).

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall respond to user interactions within 2 seconds (p95 latency) under normal operating conditions with concurrent user load up to 1000 users. Peak load response time shall not exceed 5 seconds (p95).

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The application must be user-friendly

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS - Quality Score 20/100

Review: 'User-friendly' is subjective and fails ISO 29148 verifiability requirement. Lacks measurable success criteria. Different user groups have different expectations. No usability metrics defined (task completion time, error rates, learning curve).

Recommendation: Define specific usability metrics using ISO 9241 (Ergonomics of Human-Computer Interaction). Include: task completion rate, error rate, time-on-task, System Usability Scale (SUS) score.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The application shall enable users to complete core tasks (login, search, purchase) within 3 steps with 95% success rate on first attempt. Error rate shall not exceed 2%. Average task completion time for new users shall not exceed 5 minutes.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Users can search

Assessment: INCOMPLETE - Quality Score 30/100

Review: Requirement violates ISO 29148 completeness dimension (ACTOR-ACTION-OBJECT pattern). Missing: (1) Search scope - what can users search for? (2) Search conditions - when/where? (3) Expected results - what gets returned? (4) Performance criteria - how fast? Missing actor specification and boundary conditions.

Recommendation: Implement complete ACTOR-ACTION-OBJECT-CONDITIONS structure. Specify: data domain, filtering capabilities, result set size, performance expectations, sorting/pagination.

Revision Guide: REVISED: Users shall be able to search for products by name, category, price range, and availability status. The system shall return matching results within 1 second and display up to 50 results per page with pagination controls. Search shall be case-insensitive and support partial keyword matching.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system shall return search results within 2 seconds

Assessment: CLEAR & MEASURABLE - Quality Score 78/100

Review: Requirement meets most ISO 29148 criteria: verifiable (2 seconds is measurable), specific (response time metric), actor implied (system). Strengths: quantified target, clear action, defined output. Minor gaps: (1) Load conditions not specified - 2 seconds under what user load? (2) Percentile not defined - p50 or p95? (3) Dataset size not mentioned - for how many records?

Recommendation: Enhance by adding operating conditions: specify concurrent user load, dataset size, and latency percentile. Consider network conditions and hardware baseline.

Revision Guide: ENHANCED: The system shall return search results within 2 seconds (p95 latency) for queries against datasets containing up to 1 million product records, under normal operating conditions with maximum 5000 concurrent users.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system should be secure

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS & INCOMPLETE - Quality Score 15/100

Review: 'Secure' is dangerously vague in security context. Fails ISO 29148 verifiability and completeness. Does not specify: (1) What assets are protected? (2) Against which threats? (3) What security mechanisms? (4) Which standards apply? 'Secure' could mean authentication, encryption, authorization, audit logging, or intrusion detection - each requires different implementation.

Recommendation: Decompose into domain-specific security requirements: data encryption (algorithm, key length), authentication (mechanism, MFA), authorization (access control model), audit logging, compliance standards (OWASP, NIST, ISO 27001).

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall (1) encrypt all sensitive data at rest using AES-256-GCM encryption, (2) encrypt all network traffic using TLS 1.3 or higher, (3) enforce multi-factor authentication for admin access, (4) implement role-based access control (RBAC) with principle of least privilege, and (5) maintain comprehensive audit logs of all security-relevant events with tamper-proof timestamping.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system shall allow guest checkout but users must log in before checkout

Assessment: INCONSISTENT & CONFLICTING - Quality Score 10/100

Review: Requirement contains direct logical contradiction on same topic (checkout process). Statement 1: 'allow guest checkout' (no login required). Statement 2: 'must log in before checkout' (login required). These are mutually exclusive conditions. Violates ISO 29148 consistency dimension. Will create ambiguity during implementation and testing - which behavior is correct?

Recommendation: Clarify business intent: Is guest checkout permitted or mandatory login? If both allowed, differentiate: guest vs. registered user paths. Use conditional logic: 'If user is logged in, THEN skip login. If user is guest, THEN require login OR allow guest checkout.'

Revision Guide: REVISED (Option A - Guest Allowed): The system shall permit guest checkout by accepting email and shipping address without requiring user account creation. Alternatively (Option B - Login Required): The system shall require users to either log in to existing account or create new account before completing checkout. [Select one based on business requirement]

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system should handle many users

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS & NON-VERIFIABLE - Quality Score 22/100

Review: 'Many users' is not quantifiable - could mean 100, 10,000, or 1 million. 'Handle' is vague: does it mean respond? Process? Store? Requirement fails ISO 29148 verifiability criterion. No performance degradation criteria specified. Cannot write test case without numerical target.

Recommendation: Specify exact concurrent user target, define performance baseline (response time, throughput), and degradation limits. Include peak hour projections and growth rate. Consider simultaneous connections vs. daily active users.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall support minimum 10,000 concurrent users during peak operating hours (6 PM - 10 PM). System response time shall remain within 2 seconds (p95) even at peak load. System shall be designed to scale to 50,000 concurrent users within 12 months. Database query response time shall not exceed 500ms at peak load.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Display the dashboard

Assessment: INCOMPLETE & MISSING ACTOR - Quality Score 35/100

Review: Requirement violates ACTOR-ACTION-OBJECT pattern by omitting actor. 'Display' implies passive voice. Questions: (1) Who/what initiates display? (2) When should display occur? (3) What condition triggers display? (4) To whom is dashboard displayed? Implementation cannot proceed without actor clarification.

Recommendation: Add actor (User, System, Administrator). Specify trigger event (on login, on button click, automatically on schedule). Define preconditions. Specify dashboard content/data displayed.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall display the dashboard to the user immediately upon successful authentication. Dashboard shall refresh automatically every 30 seconds and show: (1) current system status, (2) latest alerts, (3) user activity summary, and (4) performance metrics for the past 24 hours.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system should respond quickly during peak hours

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS & CONTEXT-DEPENDENT - Quality Score 28/100

Review: Contains multiple ambiguities: (1) 'quickly' is subjective - 100ms vs. 5 seconds? (2) 'peak hours' undefined - what time window? (3) Relative to what baseline? Not verifiable. Fails ISO 29148 completeness (missing load details) and verifiability (no measurable metric). Different stakeholders interpret 'quick' differently.

Recommendation: Replace subjective 'quick' with specific latency target. Define peak hours precisely (date/time range). Include concurrent user load. Consider percentile latency (p95, p99). Specify performance baseline for comparison.

Revision Guide: REVISED: During peak operating hours (6 PM to 10 PM weekdays), the system shall maintain response time for user requests within 3 seconds (p95 latency) while supporting up to 15,000 concurrent users. This represents maximum 50% performance degradation from normal-load baseline of 2 seconds response time.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system should rarely fail

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS & NON-VERIFIABLE - Quality Score 18/100

Review: 'Rarely fail' is subjective and unmeasurable. Questions: (1) How often is 'rarely'? 1 in 100 or 1 in 1 million? (2) What constitutes 'fail'? Total outage or partial degradation? (3) Over what measurement period? Per hour, day, month, year? Violates ISO 29148 verifiability. Cannot write acceptance test without numerical target.

Recommendation: Define uptime percentage using SLA format. Specify Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) and Mean Time To Recovery (MTTR). Include planned vs. unplanned downtime. Consider operational windows for maintenance.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall maintain 99.9% availability (uptime) measured on a monthly basis, allowing maximum 43.2 minutes of unplanned downtime per month. Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) shall be minimum 720 hours. Mean Time To Recovery (MTTR) from failure shall not exceed 15 minutes. Excludes scheduled maintenance windows (first Sunday of each month, 2 AM - 4 AM UTC).

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Store user data

Assessment: INCOMPLETE & AMBIGUOUS - Quality Score 25/100

Review: Missing critical context: (1) Which user data? (2) Where to store (database, cache, archive)? (3) Storage duration? (4) Encryption requirement? (5) Retention policy? (6) Who accesses? Violates ACTOR-ACTION-OBJECT completeness. No implementation guidance possible.

Recommendation: Specify data types explicitly (name, email, payment info, etc.), storage mechanism (RDBMS, NoSQL, file system), duration (session-based, persistent, archived), security (encryption), access controls (who, when), and retention/deletion policy.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall persistently store user profile data (name, email, phone, address) in encrypted form in the production database. Data shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption. Users shall retain access indefinitely until account deletion. Admin users shall have authenticated read-only access for support purposes. User data shall be automatically deleted 12 months after account closure per GDPR requirements.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system must support multiple languages

Assessment: INCOMPLETE & AMBIGUOUS - Quality Score 40/100

Review: Requirement lacks specificity: (1) How many languages? (2) Which languages? (3) What UI elements must be translated? (4) What about date/time/currency formatting? (5) Right-to-left language support? (6) Completeness of translation (100% or partial)? Can be implemented in multiple ways without clear guidance.

Recommendation: Specify language list explicitly. Define scope: UI strings only, or include help documentation, error messages, reports? Include locale data (currency, date format). Specify translation verification mechanism.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall support user interface in minimum 10 languages: English, Spanish, French, German, Chinese (Simplified), Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Italian, and Russian. All UI strings, error messages, and help documentation shall be translated. Date, time, and currency formatting shall automatically adjust to user locale. Translation completeness shall be verified at 100% before release. Users shall select preferred language during first login, with ability to change in settings.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Generate reports quickly

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS & INCOMPLETE - Quality Score 32/100

Review: Multiple ambiguities: (1) 'quickly' - 1 second or 10 seconds? (2) What type of reports? (3) What data range? Current day or full dataset? (4) Report format (PDF, Excel, CSV)? (5) Who generates? User/Admin/System? Fails verifiability and completeness requirements.

Recommendation: Specify report types and generation targets. Define performance criteria based on report size/complexity. Include timeout limits. Specify output formats. Address async generation for large reports.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall generate standard reports (daily sales summary, user activity log, system performance metrics) within 5 seconds for data spanning up to 30 days. Complex reports (annual data analysis, full dataset analysis) shall complete within 60 seconds. Reports shall be available in PDF, Excel, and CSV formats. Large reports (>10MB) shall be generated asynchronously with email notification upon completion. Report generation shall be accessible to Admin and Manager roles only.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The system validates input

Assessment: INCOMPLETE - Quality Score 38/100

Review: Missing crucial details: (1) What input fields? (2) What validation rules? (3) Error handling - how are validation failures communicated? (4) What constitutes valid input? Pattern, format, range? Cannot create test cases. Violates completeness dimension.

Recommendation: Enumerate input fields explicitly. Define validation rules per field (range, format, allowed values, length constraints). Specify error messages. Include edge cases and boundary conditions.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall validate all user input with following rules: (1) Email: RFC 5322 format, max 254 characters. Invalid: user sees error 'Please enter valid email format'. (2) Phone: numeric, 10-15 digits only. Invalid: 'Phone must contain only digits, 10-15 characters'. (3) Password: minimum 12 characters, require uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character. (4) Username: alphanumeric, 3-30 characters, no spaces. Validation errors shall appear inline near field within 200ms. Invalid form submission shall be prevented.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Provide good error messages

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS & SUBJECTIVE - Quality Score 20/100

Review: 'Good' is subjective and not measurable. No criteria for what makes error message good. Questions: (1) What constitutes 'good'? Clear language? Actionable? (2) What error scenarios? (3) Where displayed? (4) In what language? Fails verifiability and completeness. Different developers will interpret 'good' differently, leading to inconsistent implementation.

Recommendation: Define error message standards: clarity (target reading level), actionability (include solution steps), consistency (format standard), localization, visibility (where shown). Specify error message template and examples.

Revision Guide: REVISED: All error messages shall follow format: '[Error Type]: [Problem Description]. [Suggested Action]'. Messages shall be clear, non-technical, at 8th grade reading level. Examples: 'Invalid Email: The email you entered is not valid. Please check spelling and try again.' or 'Password Too Short: Password must contain at least 12 characters. Include uppercase, lowercase, number, and symbol.' Error messages shall appear within 500ms in red text, displayed inline near the offending field and in error summary at top of form.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
System backup must be automatic

Assessment: INCOMPLETE & AMBIGUOUS - Quality Score 35/100

Review: Lacks implementation details: (1) Backup frequency? (2) Data scope - full or incremental? (3) Retention period? (4) Storage location? (5) Verification/restore testing? (6) RTO and RPO targets? Meets basic intent but insufficient for DevOps/QA teams.

Recommendation: Specify frequency (hourly/daily), scope (full/incremental), retention policy, storage redundancy, verification testing frequency, and recovery targets (RTO/RPO).

Revision Guide: REVISED: The system shall perform automated daily full backup at 2:00 AM UTC with incremental backups every 4 hours. Backups shall be retained for minimum 30 days. Full backups shall be stored in geographically redundant locations (primary datacenter + secondary backup location). Recovery Point Objective (RPO) shall be maximum 4 hours of data loss. Recovery Time Objective (RTO) shall be maximum 30 minutes. Monthly backup restoration drills shall be conducted to verify backup integrity and recovery procedures.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Users should be able to recover forgotten passwords

Assessment: INCOMPLETE - Quality Score 45/100

Review: Requirement addresses key use case but lacks security and procedural details: (1) Recovery mechanism (email, security questions, SMS)? (2) Verification requirements? (3) Token expiration? (4) Account lockout after failed attempts? (5) Audit logging? Implementation could create security vulnerabilities without clear specification.

Recommendation: Specify recovery flow: trigger, verification method, token lifespan, attempt limits, success notification. Include security considerations: rate limiting, audit trail, secure token generation.

Revision Guide: REVISED: Users shall have ability to reset forgotten password via email verification. (1) Click 'Forgot Password' link. (2) Enter registered email address. (3) System sends password reset link valid for 24 hours. (4) User clicks link and creates new password (12+ chars, mixed case, digit, special char). System shall enforce: maximum 5 reset attempts per email per 24 hours, reset link one-time use only, password history (cannot reuse last 5 passwords), audit trail of all reset attempts, email confirmation of successful password change.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The API must be RESTful

Assessment: INCOMPLETE & TOO VAGUE - Quality Score 40/100

Review: 'RESTful' is architectural style but lacks implementation specifics: (1) Version control strategy? (2) Authentication method? (3) Error response formats? (4) Rate limiting? (5) Documentation requirements? (6) Versioning approach (URL vs. header)? Violates specificity and completeness. Developers need concrete specifications, not architectural directives alone.

Recommendation: Specify REST API standards explicitly: HTTP method mapping, URI patterns, response status codes, authentication (OAuth 2.0), rate limiting, error response structure, API versioning strategy, documentation format (OpenAPI/Swagger), pagination, filtering, sorting.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The API shall follow REST architectural style with: (1) HTTP methods (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE) mapping to standard CRUD operations, (2) URI format /api/v2/resources/{id}, (3) Stateless authentication via OAuth 2.0 Bearer token in Authorization header, (4) Rate limiting 1000 requests/hour per API key, (5) Error responses in format {"error": "error_code", "message": "Human readable message"}, (6) Pagination via limit (default 20, max 100) and offset parameters, (7) Full documentation in OpenAPI 3.0 Specification format, (8) Version management: maximum 2 API versions supported concurrently.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The platform must be scalable

Assessment: AMBIGUOUS & VAGUE - Quality Score 22/100

Review: 'Scalable' is subjective architectural goal, not testable requirement: (1) Scale to what user volume? (2) What scaling dimension (database, API server, storage)? (3) Horizontal or vertical? (4) Timeline for scaling? (5) Performance degradation limits? Cannot write test or acceptance criteria. Violates verifiability and completeness.

Recommendation: Decompose scalability into specific, quantified requirements: concurrent user targets at multiple growth phases, database query performance under load, storage capacity roadmap, response time thresholds, auto-scaling policies.

Revision Guide: REVISED: The platform shall be designed for horizontal scalability with following targets: (1) Phase 1 (Y1): Support 10,000 concurrent users, 100GB database, 99% uptime. (2) Phase 2 (Y2): Support 100,000 concurrent users, 500GB database, 99.9% uptime. (3) Phase 3 (Y3): Support 1,000,000 concurrent users, 5TB database, 99.95% uptime. Architecture shall use microservices, containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and database sharding. Response time shall remain <2 seconds (p95) at each phase. Auto-scaling shall add server instances when CPU utilization exceeds 70% for 5 minutes.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Notify users of important events

Assessment: INCOMPLETE & AMBIGUOUS - Quality Score 28/100

Review: Lacks critical specifications: (1) Which events qualify as 'important'? (2) Notification channels (email, SMS, push, in-app)? (3) Notification timing (real-time or batched)? (4) User preferences for notification control? (5) What information included? Violates completeness. Multiple interpretations possible leading to scope creep.

Recommendation: Define event taxonomy explicitly. Specify notification channels per event type. Include preferences mechanism. Define delivery SLAs. Include unsubscribe mechanism per event type.

Revision Guide: REVISED: System shall notify users of following events: (1) Order Confirmation - email + in-app notification within 5 minutes of order placement. (2) Shipment Alert - push notification + email within 2 hours of shipment. (3) Price Drop - push notification within 10 minutes of 15%+ price reduction for wishlist items. (4) Security Alert (login from new location) - immediate push + SMS notification. Users shall have granular notification preferences, able to enable/disable each event type per channel. Users shall have unsubscribe link in every email. SMS notifications limited to critical security events unless opted-in for additional notifications. Delivery failure shall retry up to 3 times over 24 hours.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
The database should be optimized

Assessment: VAGUE & NON-VERIFIABLE - Quality Score 18/100

Review: 'Optimized' is qualitative goal, not testable requirement: (1) Optimize for what metric (query speed, storage, throughput)? (2) Target performance baseline? (3) Which queries or operations? (4) Acceptable response times? Cannot measure success. Violates ISO 29148 verifiability criterion completely.

Recommendation: Replace with performance targets: query response times per operation type, throughput capacity (queries per second), storage efficiency (compression ratio), resource utilization (CPU, memory). Include indexing and caching strategies if relevant.

Revision Guide: REVISED: Database performance shall meet following targets: (1) User login query: <50ms response time (p95). (2) Product search: <500ms response time (p95) for 1-million record dataset. (3) Order history retrieval: <200ms response time (p95). (4) Bulk analytics query: <30 seconds for 12-month data range. (5) Database operations shall support minimum 1000 queries per second (QPS). Storage shall be optimized through indexing on frequently-queried columns (user_id, order_date, product_id) and regular query plan analysis. Connection pooling shall maintain 20-100 active connections. Database query logging shall capture queries >1 second for optimization review.

You are a senior software requirements analyst following ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148. Analyze software requirements professionally. Always respond in 4 structured parts: 1. Assessment 2. Review 3. Recommendation 4. Revision Guide. Be specific, critical, and avoid vague explanations. Ensure suggestions are measurable and testable.
Mobile app must work on smartphones

Assessment: INCOMPLETE & AMBIGUOUS - Quality Score 32/100

Review: Vague scope: (1) Which smartphone platforms (iOS, Android, or both)? (2) Minimum OS versions? (3) Supported device sizes/screen resolutions? (4) Feature parity with web version or limited features? (5) Offline functionality? (6) Tablet support? Fails completeness and specificity. Leads to mismatched expectations between stakeholders.

Recommendation: Specify platform support matrix: OS versions, device types, screen sizes. Define feature set. Include performance targets for mobile networks (3G/4G/5G). Specify supported capabilities (camera, GPS, etc.).

Revision Guide: REVISED: Mobile applications shall support: (1) iOS: minimum iOS 13.0 on iPhone, iPad (2) Android: minimum Android 10.0. (2) Supported devices: smartphones (screen size 3.5-6.7 inches) and tablets (7-13 inches). (3) Feature parity with web version for core functions: login, search, purchase, order tracking. (4) Responsive UI adapting to all supported screen densities (LDPI, MDPI, HDPI, XHDPI, XXHDPI, XXXHDPI). (5) Performance targets on mobile: load screen within 3 seconds on 4G network, response time <2 seconds for user interactions. (6) Minimum offline capability: cached data from last session. App update checks daily via app store.
